name: com.coachfit.calendar.adapter.in.CalendarController
comment:
REST controller for the calendar module endpoints. 
 GET    /api/v1/calendar?from=...&to=...  — events in date range (flat list) POST   /api/v1/calendar                  — create event PUT    /api/v1/calendar/{id}             — update event fields DELETE /api/v1/calendar/{id}             — soft delete PUT    /api/v1/calendar/{id}/complete    — mark partial → completed PUT    /api/v1/calendar/{id}/skip        — planned → skipped (or reverse) POST   /api/v1/calendar/reorder         — reorder same-day events

All endpoints require authentication. Ownership enforcement is in the service layer.

Coach assignment is out of scope for this ticket — {@code assignedBy} is always null.

methods.name: list
methods.comment:
Returns all calendar events in the given date range (inclusive, flat list).

Query parameters:

  • {@code from} — start date (ISO-8601, required)
  • {@code to} — end date (ISO-8601, required)

Max range is limited to 366 days to avoid unbounded queries.

methods.name: create
methods.comment:
Creates a new calendar event for the authenticated user.

Validation:

  • {@code date}, {@code eventType}, and {@code title} are required.
  • {@code eventType} must be one of: workout, note, race, rest.
Returns 201 with a {@code Location} header pointing to the created resource.

methods.name: update
methods.comment:
Updates the mutable fields of an existing calendar event. Returns 200 with the updated event.

Note: status transitions (complete, skip) use dedicated sub-resource endpoints.

methods.name: delete
methods.comment: Soft-deletes a calendar event (sets {@code deleted_at}). Returns 204 No Content on success.

methods.name: complete
methods.comment:
Manually transitions a {@code partial} event to {@code completed}.

State machine: {@code partial} → {@code completed} only. Returns 409 if the event is in any other state.

methods.name: skip
methods.comment:
Marks a {@code planned} event as {@code skipped}. When the optional body {@code { "unskip": true }} is present, transitions a {@code skipped} event back to {@code planned}.

Returns 409 if the current state does not allow the transition.

methods.name: reorder
methods.comment:
Reorders calendar events on the same day by reassigning {@code order_index}.

The client sends an ordered list of event IDs. Positions in the list become the new {@code order_index} values (0-based). Events not owned by the user are silently skipped.

methods.name: syncToGarmin
methods.comment:
Pushes a workout calendar event to the user's Garmin Connect calendar.

Pre-conditions:

  • The event must be a {@code workout} event with an attached {@code workout_id}.
  • The user must have an active Garmin OAuth connection.
  • CoachFit must have Garmin Training API access.

Response 200 with Garmin IDs: 

{@code { "garminWorkoutId":   "12345", "garminScheduledId": "67890", "scheduledDate":     "2025-03-20" } }

Returns 400 if the event has no workout. Returns 503 if Garmin API is unavailable.

methods.name: removeFromGarmin
methods.comment:
Removes a previously synced workout from the user's Garmin Connect calendar.

No-op (200 OK) if the event was never synced to Garmin.

name: com.coachfit.calendar.adapter.in.dto.CalendarEventResponse
comment:
Response DTO for all calendar event endpoints.

Matches the JSON shape documented in docs/05-api-design.md §Calendar: 

 { "id": "uuid", "date": "2025-03-15", "eventType": "workout", "title": "Tempo Intervals", "status": "completed", "workout": { "id": "uuid", "sport": "cycling", "estimatedDuration": 3600 }, "activity": { "id": "uuid", "tss": 75.5, "durationSeconds": 3650 }, "complianceScore": 92.5, "orderIndex": 0, "assignedBy": null }
